Question: Where was Moses when he spoke to Israel?
Response: Moses was east of the Jordan in the wilderness, in the plain of the Jordan River valley.

Question: When did Moses speak to the people of Israel, telling them all that Yahweh commanded him?
Response: Moses spoke to the people in the fortieth year after they had left Egypt.

Question: To whom had Yahweh promised to give the land?
Response: Yahweh had promised to give the land to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob and to their descendants.

Question: Why did Moses feel he could not govern the people alone?
Response: There were too many of them since God had multiplied them and made them as numerous as the stars of the heavens.

Question: Who would help Moses solve all the problems and disputes of the people?
Response: The people would choose wise, understanding, and well-known men from each tribe and appoint these men leaders to help Moses.

Question: What did Moses command the judges to do?
Response: He commanded them to hear and judge righteously concerning the quarrels and cases the Israelites had with each other or with any foreigner who lived among them.

Question: Where did Israel travel through as they journeyed from Horeb to the hill country?
Response: They traveled through all the great and terrible wilderness.

Question: What did the people ask Moses to do before they would enter the land?
Response: They asked Moses to send men ahead to explore the land and report back about it.

Question: Who did Moses choose to scout out the land?
Response: He chose one man from each of the 12 tribes.

Question: What did the men report back about the land?
Response: They reported that the land that Yahweh was giving them was good.

Question: What were the people grumbling against Yahweh about?
Response: They said Yahweh hated them and he brought them out of the land of Egypt to destroy them.

Question: What did the spies say about the people of the land?
Response: They said that the people of the land were stronger and taller than the Israelites and had fortified cities.

Question: What did Moses say was the reason the people did not need to be afraid?
Response: He told them not to be afraid, because Yahweh would go before them and would fight for them like he did in Egypt and in the wilderness.

Question: What did Yahweh say when he became angry?
Response: He said that not one of these men would see the good land except Caleb.

Question: What did Yahweh tell Moses to do for Joshua, the son of Nun?
Response: Yahweh told Moses to encourage Joshua for he would lead Israel to occupy the land.

Question: Who did Yahweh say will go into the land and possess it?
Response: He said their children will go in and will possess the land.

Question: What did the people do when Yahweh told them not to attack the hill country?
Response: They were arrogant and went up to the hill country to fight anyway.

Question: Where did Moses say they journeyed?
Response: They journeyed into the wilderness on the way to the Sea of Reeds.

Question: What did Yehweh command the people to be careful not to do?
Response: They were not to fight the descendants of Esau, who live near Mount Seir, for Yahweh had given that land to them.

Question: How were they to get food and water?
Response: They were to buy food and water from the people living there so they could eat and drink.

Question: What did Yahweh tell them they were not to do in Moab?
Response: They were not to trouble or fight with the people of Moab because Yahweh had given that land to them.

Question: How long did it take Israel to go from Kadesh Barnea to the wadi of Zered?
Response: It took them 38 years from when they left until they crossed wadi of Zered.

Question: What happened to all the men who had been old enough to fight?
Response: All the men who had been old enough to fight had died.

Question: To whom had Yahweh given the land where the people of Ammon were living?
Response: Yahweh had given that land to the descendants of Lot.

Question: What will the people of the land do when they hear news about the Israelites?
Response: They will tremble and be afraid because of what they hear about the Israelites.

Question: What words of peace did Moses send to Sihon, king of Heshbon?
Response: Moses asked that they be allowed to pass through his land, staying on the road and not turning to the right or left.

Question: How did Moses plan to get food and water for the people?
Response: He asked the king to sell him food and water so the Israelites could eat and drink.

Question: What happened when Sihon came out to fight against Moses and his people?
Response: Yahweh allowed Moses and his people to defeated him, killing him, his son, and all his people.

Question: What happened to all the king’s cities?
Response: Moses captured all the king’s cities and destroyed them, including women and children, leaving no survivors.

Question: What did Og, the king of Bashan, do to Israel as they approached his land?
Response: As Israel approached the land of Bashan, the king and all his soldiers came and attacked them.

Question: What did Israel do to the king of Bashan and his people?
Response: They defeated the king of Bashan, killing him and all his people, leaving no survivors.

Question: What did Israel do with the cattle and the spoil of the cities?
Response: They took for themselves all the cattle and the spoil of the cities.

Question: To whom did Moses give the land that they had captured?
Response: He gave it to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and half of the tribe of Manasseh.

Question: What did Moses command the men of war of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and half of the tribe of Manasseh to do?
Response: He commanded them to cross over the Jordan River to help the rest of the people of Israel conquer that land.

Question: When did Yahweh say the Reubenites, the Gadites, and half of the tribe of Manasseh could return to their own land?
Response: Yahweh said they could return to their own land after the rest of the Israelites captured the land that God was giving to them.

Question: What did Moses tell Joshua not to do?
Response: He told Joshua not to fear the enemy because Yahweh would be the one who would fight for him.

Question: What did Moses ask Yahweh to allow him to do?
Response: He asked Yahweh to let him go see the good land across the Jordan River and in Lebanon.

Question: How did Yahweh respond to Moses’ request to go and see the land?
Response: Yahweh was angry with Moses because of what the people had done and did not listen to his request.

Question: What did Yahweh allow Moses to do?
Response: Yahweh told him to go up to the top of Mount Pisgah and look all around from there, for he would not cross the Jordan River.

Question: What did Yahweh tell Moses to do for Joshua?
Response: He told Moses to instruct, encourage, and strengthen Joshua, because he would lead the people across the river to occupy the land that he could see.

Question: What must the people of Israel do so that they may live and go in and possess the land that Yahweh is giving them?
Response: They must listen and obey the laws that Moses will teach them so that they may possess the land.

Question: What does Moses instruct the people not to do?
Response: They must not add to or take away from the words of the commands Moses is giving them so they will obey the commandments of Yahweh.

Question: What happened to the men who were worshiping Baal?
Response: Yahweh destroyed the men that worshiped the Baal of Peor.

Question: What will be the result of Israel keeping the rules that Moses commanded them?
Response: The people around them will notice that because they keep these rules, they are a nation that has wisdom and understanding.

Question: What should the people do so that they do not forget what God has done?
Response: They should guard themselves by remembering the things God has done and telling them to their children and their grandchildren.

Question: What did the people hear out of the middle of the fire on the mountain?
Response: They heard Yahweh’s voice out of the middle of the fire but did not see him.

Question: What was the covenant written on that Yahweh commanded them to obey?
Response: The Ten Commandments that Yahweh gave to Moses for the people to obey were written on two tablets of stone.

Question: What were the people told not to do so as to not act corruptly?
Response: They were told not to make any carved figures or idols of any thing.

Question: Why did Moses tell the people to be careful about the sun, moon, and stars?
Response: He told them that they should be careful to not be seduced to worship the heavens, sun, moon, or stars.

Question: Why did Yahweh bring the people out of Egypt?
Response: He brought them out of Egypt to be a people who belong to him.

Question: What did Yahweh declare to Moses because he was angry with him?
Response: He declared that Moses would not cross the Jordan River into the good land.

Question: Why did Yahweh forbid Israel to make any carved figure or idol?
Response: Yahweh their God is a jealous God and wants them to worship only him.

Question: What does Moses say will happen if the people corrupt themselves and do evil in Yahweh’s sight?
Response: They will perish in the land over the Jordan and be completely destroyed.

Question: What does Moses say Yahweh will do to the people if they worship idols?
Response: Yahweh will scatter them among other nations and only a few would survive.

Question: When will the people find Yahweh?
Response: They will find him when they search for him with all their heart and all their soul.

Question: What will make the people return to Yahweh their God and listen to his voice?
Response: When they are in trouble and when all the bad things have come on them, they will return to Yahweh.

Question: What did God do for the people when they were in Egypt?
Response: God chose for himself all these people from the midst of another nation, by using his great power when he performed miracles, sent plagues, and fought the Egyptians for them.

Question: What did Yahweh do to show the people that he alone is God?
Response: He allowed them to hear his voice speak from heaven, and on earth he made them see his great fire and hear his voice coming from it.

Question: What did Yahweh do for Israel because he loved their ancestors?
Response: He chose their descendants and brought them out of Egypt with his great power. He gave them their land after driving out the more powerful nations.

Question: Where could a person flee if he killed another person accidently?
Response: He could flee to one of the three cities that Moses had selected on the east side of the Jordan River.

Question: What was included in the law that Moses gave the people of Israel?
Response: He gave them the law which included all the instructions, rules, and regulations that he told them when they came out of Egypt.

Question: From which two kings did Moses and his people take land on the east side of the Jordan River?
Response: The Israelites took the land of Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan.

Question: Where did Yahweh make his covenant with the people?
Response: Yahweh made his covenant with the people of Israel at Horeb.

Question: Why did Moses stand between Yahweh and the people when Yahweh revealed his word to them?
Response: They were afraid of the fire from which Yahweh was speaking so Moses stood between them in order to tell them what he said.

Question: Who does Yahweh tell Israel he is?
Response: He told them that he is Yahweh their God, who brought them from the land of Egypt and freed them from slavery.

Question: What does God tell Israel not make, bow down to, or serve?
Response: They should not make carved figures of any likeness of anything that is in the sky, on earth, or in the water. If they do, God will punish them and their descendants.

Question: What will Yahweh do for those who love him and keep his commandments?
Response: He will show covenant faithfulness to those who love him and obey his commandments.

Question: Who will Yahweh not leave unpunished?
Response: He will not leave unpunished those who use his name for wrong purposes.

Question: What does Yahweh say about the seventh day?
Response: The seventh day is a day of rest which is dedicated to Yahweh their God, so they must not do any work.

Question: What does Yahweh want them to remember when they observe the Sabbath day?
Response: He wants them to remember that they were slaves in Egypt and that God brought them out by his great power.

Question: What did Yahweh say would be the result if they honor their father and mother?
Response: They will live a long time in the land that God gave them and things will go well for them there.

Question: What were they commanded not to do?
Response: They should not murder, commit adultery, steal, or give false witness.

Question: On what did Yahweh write down his words?
Response: He wrote his words down on two tablets of stone and gave them to Moses.

Question: What did the people say would happen to them if they heard the voice of Yahweh any longer?
Response: The people said that they would be burned up by the great fire and would die.

Question: What did the people say they would do when Moses tells them everything that Yahweh says?
Response: They said they will listen and obey Yahweh’s word.

Question: What did Yahweh say about the response of the people?
Response: Yahweh said he heard what the people said and that what they had spoken was good.

Question: What did Moses say would happen to the people if they did all that Yahweh had commanded them?
Response: If they keep the commandments, they will live a long time in the land and things will go well for them there.

Question: What will happen if the Israelites and their children keep all Yahweh’s statutes and commandments all the days of your lives?
Response: If they keep his statutes and commandments, they will live for a long time.

Question: How did Moses describe the land into which the people were going?
Response: He said that it was a land flowing with milk and honey.

Question: How should Israel love Yahweh?
Response: They should love Yahweh with all their heart, all their soul, and all their strength.

Question: When should the people diligently teach the commandments to their children?
Response: They should teach the commandments to their children at every opportunity such as when they are in their house or walking on the road, lying down to rest, or getting up.

Question: When should Israel remember what Yahweh has done for them?
Response: They should remember that it was Yahweh who brought them into the land he had promised them when they are enjoying every good thing that Yahweh had given them that they did not have to work for.

Question: What will happen to Israel if they worship the gods of the people around them?
Response: Yahweh will become angry with them and he will destroy them completely if they worship the gods of the people around them.

Question: What did the people do at Massah that they should not repeat?
Response: They tested Yahweh by doing sinful things to see if he would punish them or not.

Question: How should the people respond when their children ask about all the things that Yahweh commanded?
Response: They should tell their children about how they were Pharaoh’s slaves and Yahweh brought them out of Egypt and gave them the land that he promised to their ancestors.

Question: Why should the people fear Yahweh?
Response: They should fear him so that things will go well for them and so that he will protect them as a nation.

Question: What must the people do to the people of the land that Yahweh is bringing them into?
Response: They must defeat them and destroy them completely.

Question: What must the Israelites not do with the people of those nations?
Response: They are not to make any covenants with them, show them mercy, or arrange any marriages with them for their sons or daughters.

Question: How should Israel deal with anything associated with the idols of the people of these nations?
Response: They should break down their altars, smash their sacred stone pillars, cut down their Asherah poles, and burn their idols.

Question: Did Yahweh keep his oath to their ancestors?
Response: Yahweh kept his oath and rescued them from slavery to Pharaoh in Egypt.

Question: What will Yahweh do with those who love him and keep his commandments?
Response: Yahweh will keep his covenants with those who love him and keep his commandments and faithfully love them forever.

Question: In what ways will the people be blessed by Yahweh if they keep his decrees?
Response: God will love them and bless them and multiply them by giving them many children, crops, and animals.

Question: What will God do to the nations of whom Israel is afraid?
Response: God will make them experience the same kinds of great trials and other displays of his power that they saw in Egypt.

Question: Why should the Israelites not be frightened of the people?
Response: Yahweh, who is a great and fearsome God, will be with them.

Question: How will God enable the people to defeat their enemies?
Response: God will greatly confuse them in battle and cause them to panic until they are destroyed.

Question: What are they to do to the idols of the gods of the nations?
Response: They are to burn the idols and not even keep the gold or silver they are made of, as they are to be completely destroyed.

Question: What should the people of Israel remember?
Response: They should remember all the ways that Yahweh led them over those 40 years in the wilderness. He was testing them to find out if they would obey his commandments or not.

Question: How and why did Yahweh humble them?
Response: He allowed them to be hungry and then fed them manna so that that they would know that they could not live only by having enough food to eat but also by paying attention to everything that Yahweh says.

Question: How else did Yahweh provide for them all those years in the wilderness?
Response: Their clothing did not wear out and their feet did not swell.

Question: How did Yahweh instruct the Israelites?
Response: As a man instructs and corrects his son, so Yahweh taught and corrected them.

Question: What is in the good land into which God is bringing them?
Response: The land will be filled with many sources of water, grain, fruit and olive trees, and honey.

Question: What will cause the people to bless and thank Yahweh?
Response: They will thank him because of the good and fertile land that he has given them.

Question: What were the people reminded not to do?
Response: They were reminded not to forget Yahweh by failing to obey his commandments.

Question: Why might the people forget Yahweh who brought them out of Egypt?
Response: When all that they possess increases, including herds, flock, and silver and gold, they might become proud and they might forget Yahweh.

Question: How did Yahweh provide water and food in the terrible wilderness for the people of Israel?
Response: Yahweh brought water out of solid rock for them to drink and provided manna for them to eat.

Question: What would cause the people to perish like the nations around them?
Response: They will perish if they forget their God and worship and serve other gods, because they would not listen to the voice of Yahweh.

Question: Where are the people of Israel preparing to go?
Response: Israel is about to cross over the Jordan.

Question: What will happen when Israel crosses Jordan?
Response: Yahweh will go ahead of them and will destroy the nations like a devouring fire.

Question: What did Moses tell the people not to think after Yahweh enables them to drive their enemies out of the land?
Response: He told them not to think that it was because of Israel’s righteousness that Yahweh gave them the land.

Question: What is the reason God is driving the nations out?
Response: He is driving them out because of their wickedness, and to keep his promise to Israel’s ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

Question: Why was Yahweh angry with the people of Israel?
Response: He was angry with them because they were rebellious against him.

Question: What did Moses eat and drink on the mountain?
Response: He did not eat or drink anything for 40 days and 40 nights on the mountain.

Question: Why did Yahweh tell Moses to quickly go down from the mountain?
Response: Yahweh told him the people had corrupted themselves by doing what Yahweh had commanded them not to do and had made for themselves an idol to worship.

Question: Why did Yahweh say he would destroy the Israelites?
Response: Yahweh said he had seen that they were a stubborn people.

Question: What did Moses find that Israel had made while he was on the mountain?
Response: They had sinned against Yahweh by molding a calf as an idol just as he had commanded them not to do.

Question: What did Moses do after he saw that the people had done evil in the sight of Yahweh?
Response: He threw down the tablets and broke them before their eyes, he prostrated himself before Yahweh for 40 days and nights, and did not eat or drink because of their sin.

Question: Why did Moses pray for the people and Aaron?
Response: He prayed because Yahweh was angry enough with the people and with Aaron to destroy them.

Question: What did Moses do to the calf that the people had made?
Response: Moses burned it, crushed it, and ground it into dust and threw the dust into the stream that came down from the mountain.

Question: How did the people provoke Yahweh to anger?
Response: They rebelled against the commandment of Yahweh by not trusting him or obeying what he told them.

Question: What was Moses praying as he prostrated himself before Yahweh for 40 days and nights?
Response: He prayed that Yahweh would not destroy his people whom he had rescued and brought out of Egypt.

Question: What was Moses thinking the people of Egypt would say if Yahweh destroyed his people?
Response: He is afraid the people of Egypt would say that Yahweh was not able to bring Israel into the land he had promised them but that he took them into the wilderness to kill them because he hated them.

Question: Where did Yahweh tell Moses to place the two tablets of stone on which he would write the words that were on the first tablets?
Response: Yahweh told him he to place the two tablets of stone into the chest of wood that Moses would make.

Question: What did Yahweh write on the tablets of stone when Moses went up the mountain with them?
Response: Yahweh wrote the same Ten Commandments on the tablets that he had written on the first tablets.

Question: Where did Moses put the two tablets when he returned from the mountain?
Response: He put them into the chest that he had made as Yahweh commanded him.

Question: What happened in Moserah?
Response: Aaron died and was buried in Moserah and Eleazar, his son, took his place as priest.

Question: What tribe had Yahweh chosen to carry the box of the covenant and to serve Yahweh?
Response: Yahweh chose the tribe of Levi to carry the chest, to serve him, and to bless people in his name.

Question: How long did Moses stay on the mountain this time?
Response: He stayed on the mountain 40 days and 40 nights, just like he did the first time.

Question: What does Moses say that Yahweh requires of Israel?
Response: Yahweh requires Israel to fear him, walk in all his ways, love him, worship him with all their heart and soul, and to obey his commandments.

Question: What did Moses tell the people to circumcise?
Response: He told them to humble themselves as if they were circumcising the foreskin of their hearts.

Question: Why should Israel love the foreigner?
Response: Israel should love the foreigner because they were once foreigners in the land of Egypt.

Question: How has God blessed the people since they first went down into Egypt?
Response: There were only 70 of their ancestors who went down into Egypt but now, God has made them as numerous as the stars of the heavens.

Question: What does Moses tell the people to always keep?
Response: He tells them to always keep Yahweh’s rules, statutes, decrees, and commandments.

Question: Who had not seen all that Yahweh did in Egypt?
Response: The children of the Israelites who were born while they wandered in the wilderness had not seen Yahweh’s discipline and his mighty deeds that he did in Egypt.

Question: How did Yahweh destroy the army of Egypt when they pursued after Israel?
Response: Yahweh caused the water of the Sea of Reeds to drown the army of Egypt as they pursued after Israel.

Question: What had the Israelites seen?
Response: The Israelites had seen all the great works that Yahweh did.

Question: What will happen to the people if they keep all the commandments?
Response: If they keep all the commandments, they will be strong, go in and possess the land, and they and their descendants will live in the land for a long time.

Question: How is the land they are going to enter different from the land of Egypt?
Response: In the land they are going to enter there are many hills and valleys and plenty of water from the rain.

Question: What would Yahweh give them if they obey his commandments and to love and serve him with all their heart and soul?
Response: Yahweh will give them rain in its season, that they may gather their grain and be able to make wine and oil.

Question: What will Yahweh do if the people turn aside and worship other gods?
Response: He will become angry and will restrain the heavens so there will not be any rain.

Question: What should the people do so that they will live for a very long time in the land?
Response: They will write the commandments on the doorposts of their house and on their gates.

Question: If the people keep all the commandments of Yahweh and cling to him, what will he do for them?
Response: Yahweh will drive out all the other nations that are larger and mightier than they are.

Question: Why would no man be able to stand before Israel?
Response: Yahweh will cause the people of that land to fear and feel terror of Israel.

Question: What did Moses give Israel that day?
Response: He gave them a blessing and a curse.

Question: Where must the people go in order to possess the land?
Response: They must cross over the Jordan to possess and live in the land.

Question: What places did Moses tell Israel to destroy?
Response: They were to destroy all the places where the nations that Israel dispossessed had worshiped their gods.

Question: What does Yahweh instruct the people of Israel do to the name of the gods of the nations?
Response: He told Israel to destroy everything to do with those gods in order to remove their name from there.

Question: In what place were the people of Israel to bring their burnt offerings, sacrifices, tithes, and offerings?
Response: They were to go to the place that Yahweh their God would choose.

Question: What kinds of things were the people of Israel doing?
Response: They were doing whatever they thought was right.

Question: What kind of rest will Yahweh give to Israel when they go over the Jordan?
Response: He will give them rest by protecting them from all their surrounding enemies, so they will live in safety.

Question: What animals can Israel kill and eat?
Response: Israel can kill and eat domesticated animals where they are living.

Question: What was Israel not allowed to eat?
Response: Israel was not allowed to eat the blood, but must pour it out on the earth.

Question: Where was Israel told they could not eat their tithes and sacrifices?
Response: They were not allowed to eat what was intended for tithing or sacrificing where they lived.

Question: Where were the people of Israel to eat their tithes and sacrifices?
Response: They were to eat these offerings in the place that Yahweh their God will choose.

Question: What was Israel told be sure not to do?
Response: They were told to not forget to take care of the Levites.

Question: What can Israel eat when Yahweh their God enlarges their borders as he has promised them?
Response: Israel can eat the meat from the new land.

Question: What can Israel eat if the place that Yahweh their God chooses to put his name is too far from them?
Response: If the place that Yahweh Israel’s God chooses to put his name is too far from where they live, then they may kill some of their flock for sacrifices as well as to eat.

Question: Where did they consider the life of the animal?
Response: Israel was to consider the blood as the life of the animal and thus should not eat it with the meat.

Question: What is Israel instructed to take to the place that Yahweh chooses?
Response: Israel is to take the sacred things that they have and their offerings to the place that Yahweh chooses.

Question: What is to be done with the blood and the meat of the animals they offer as a burnt offering?
Response: They are to pour the blood out on the altar of Yahweh their God and eat the meat.

Question: What is Israel instructed to do so that it may go well with them and with their descendants after them?
Response: Israel is to listen to and obey all the words that Yahweh commands them and do what is good and right so that things may go well for them and their descendants forever.

Question: What does Yahweh instruct Israel to watch out for when God enables Israel to go in and dispossess the nations in the land and live there?
Response: Israel is to pay attention so that they are not trapped into serving the gods of those nations.

Question: What have the other nations done to their sons and daughters in order to worship the gods?
Response: The other nations have even burned their sons and daughters on altars to these other gods.

Question: Why should the people of Israel not listen to a prophet who predicts something that happens and then says that they should go after other gods and worship them?
Response: The people of Israel should not listen because Yahweh is testing them to know whether they love him with all their heart and with all their soul.

Question: What should the people of Israel do to the prophet who tells them to worship other gods?
Response: They should put that prophet to death because he has spoken against what Yahweh has commanded them.

Question: What have the people of Israel been instructed to do to anyone who secretly entices them and says that they should go and worship other gods?
Response: They must surely kill anyone who entices them to worship other gods.

Question: How should the people of Israel kill the person who tells them to disobey Yahweh their God?
Response: They should stone that person to death.

Question: What shall the people of Israel do if they hear that some wicked person is telling others to go and worship other gods?
Response: They should investigate it thoroughly to see if it is true.

Question: What should Israel do to a city if they learn that it is true that the inhabitants have been worshiping other gods?
Response: They should kill the inhabitants of that city and destroy everything in it.

Question: What are the people told about the things that Yahweh has told them to destroy?
Response: They must not take any of those things for themselves that Yahweh has told them to destroy, so that Yahweh will not be angry and will have compassion on them as he promised their ancestors.

Question: What does Moses instruct the people of Yahweh not to do?
Response: They are not to cut themselves nor shave any part of their heads for the dead.

Question: What are the two characteristics of the animals that the Israelites are allowed to eat?
Response: Israel may eat any animal that has split hooves and also chews the cud.

Question: Why is the pig not to be eaten?
Response: The pig is unclean for the Israelites because he has split hooves but does not chew the cud.

Question: What are the characteristics of the things from the water that the Israelites are allowed to eat?
Response: They may eat whatever has fins and scales.

Question: Are the Israelites allowed to eat winged swarming things?
Response: All winged, swarming insects are unclean for the Israelites and must not be eaten.

Question: May the Israelites eat anything that dies of natural causes?
Response: They may not eat anything that dies naturally, but they may give it to a foreigner to eat or sell.

Question: What could the Israelites not do with a young goat?
Response: They must not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.

Question: What are the Israelites to do with the crops produced in their fields?
Response: Israelites must set apart a tenth of all their crops once a year.

Question: What are the Israelites instructed to eat in the place that he will choose?
Response: Israel must eat the tithe, which was a tenth of their grain, new wine, and of their oil, as well as the firstborn animals in the place that Yahweh will choose.

Question: What are the Israelites allowed to do if they are blessed with such a large crop that they are not able to carry the tithe to the place that Yahweh will choose?
Response: If the journey is too for them to carry all of the tithe of what Yahweh has blessed them with, they can sell a tenth of their crops and take the money to the place that Yahweh has chosen.

Question: Who has no inheritance with the Israelites and must not be neglected?
Response: The Levites will not own any land so the others must not neglect to help them.

Question: What are the Israelites instructed to do at the end of every three years?
Response: At the end of every three years the Israelites are to store the tithe of their produce from that year for the Levites, the foreigners, the fatherless, and the widows to have enough to eat.

Question: What must the Israelites do at the end of every seven years?
Response: At the end of every seven years the Israelites must cancel all debts.

Question: Why are debts to be canceled every seven years?
Response: Everyone will cancel that which he has loaned to a fellow Israelite because Yahweh has declared the cancelation of debts every seven years.

Question: From whom can Israelites continue to collect payment at the end of every seven years?
Response: The Israelites can continue to collect payment of a debt from a foreigner.

Question: Why should there be no poor among the Israelites?
Response: If they do all that Yahweh has commanded them, there should no longer be poor among them since Yahweh will bless them.

Question: What will happen to demonstrate that Yahweh has blessed Israel as he promised?
Response: Israel will lend to many nations, but will not borrow; Israel will rule over many nations, but the other nations will not rule over them.

Question: What are the Israelites told not to do if there is a poor man among them?
Response: The Israelites are told not to be selfish and refuse to help them.

Question: What should the Israelites be careful not to do when the seventh year, the year of remission is near?
Response: They should not refuse to give help to a poor man because the year of canceling debts is near.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the Israelites in return for giving generously to the poor?
Response: Yahweh will bless the Israelites in everything they do.

Question: Why is Israel commanded to give generously to their fellow Israelites, to the needy and the poor in their land?
Response: They should give generously because there will always be poor living among them.

Question: What are Israelites instructed to give a Hebrew man or a Hebrew woman who is released to go free in the seventh year?
Response: They must provide for that person freely out of their flocks and their crops with which Yahweh their God has blessed them.

Question: What are the Israelites to remember as they give to the one they set free?
Response: The Israelites are told to remember that they were slaves in the land of Egypt and Yahweh freed them.

Question: What is an Israelite to do to a male or female slave who says that they do not want to leave?
Response: The Israelite is to pierce the slave’s ear, and he or she will be their slave forever.

Question: Why should it not seem difficult for the Israelite to let the slave go free?
Response: It should not be difficult because he has served them for six years and given them twice the value of a hired person.

Question: What must the people of Israel do with the firstborn males in their herd and their flock?
Response: They must set them aside for Yahweh and not use them for work or shear them.

Question: What are the Israelites to do with the firstborn animal if it is lame or blind or has any other defect?
Response: They must not sacrifice it to Yahweh, but instead they can eat it.

Question: Why are the Israelites to keep the Passover for Yahweh their God in the month of Aviv?
Response: The Israelites are to celebrate the Passover each year during the month of Aviv because Yahweh brought Israel out of Egypt in that month.

Question: What will the Israelites do to celebrate the Passover?
Response: The Israelites will sacrifice some of the flock and the herd in the place that Yahweh will choose.

Question: Why are the Israelites to eat no leavened bread or have yeast in all their land for seven days?
Response: They are to eat no leavened bread or have yeast during those days to remind them that they came out of the land of Egypt with too much haste for bread to rise.

Question: What are the Israelites told about the meat that they sacrifice in the evening on the first day?
Response: The Israelites are to make sure there is no meat that they sacrifice in the evening remaining the next morning.

Question: Where are the Israelites to offer the sacrifice of the Passover?
Response: They are to sacrifice at the place that Yahweh will choose.

Question: When will the sacrifice of the Passover be performed?
Response: The sacrifice of the Passover is to be performed in the evening when the sun is setting, at the time when the Israelites started to leave Egypt.

Question: What are the Israelites supposed to do on the first six days as they celebrate Passover?
Response: The Israelites are to eat unleavened bread for six days.

Question: What are the Israelites supposed to do on the seventh day as they celebrate Passover?
Response: On the seventh day, they are to have a festive assembly and do no work.

Question: How do the Israelites determine the start of the Festival of Weeks for Yahweh?
Response: The Israelites will count seven weeks from the time they begin to harvest their grain.

Question: What contribution do the Israelites give on the Festival of Weeks?
Response: They must give Yahweh a freewill offering according to how much Yahweh has blessed them.

Question: What must they remember during the Festival of Weeks?
Response: They must remember that they were slaves in Egypt.

Question: What festival are the Israelites instructed to keep for seven days after they have gathered in the harvest from their threshing floors and from their wine presses?
Response: They are to keep the Festival of Shelters for seven days after they have gathered in their harvest.

Question: At what the three festivals must all the males of Israel gather to worship Yahweh at the place that Yahweh chooses?
Response: They must gather to worship Yahweh at the Festival of Unleavened Bread, the Festival of Weeks, and at the Festival of Shelters.

Question: How much is every male Israelite supposed to give to Yahweh instead of coming without an offering?
Response: Every male Israelite is to give in proportion to how much Yahweh has blessed him with that year.

Question: Where are the Israelites to find their judges and officers?
Response: The judges and officers will be taken from each of their tribes to judge the people with righteous judgment.

Question: Why are the judges not to show partiality or take a bribe?
Response: If a judge takes a bribe, he will find it difficult to judge fairly but will instead want to do what the person who bribes him wants him to do.

Question: Why must the judges pursue righteousness and judge fairly?
Response: They must pursue righteousness and judge fairly, so that they may live and occupy the land that Yahweh is giving them.

Question: What are the Israelites not to set up for themselves?
Response: They are not to set up an Asherah pole or a stone pillar to worship any idol.

Question: Why must the Israelites not sacrifice an ox or a sheep with any blemish or defect to Yahweh?
Response: A sacrifice that is not perfect will be detestable to Yahweh.

Question: What are the Israelites told to do if they hear that a man or woman within their cities have done what is evil in the sight of Yahweh and is serving other gods?
Response: The Israelites are to investigate thoroughly to know if this is true and that the idol worship is actually happening.

Question: What are the Israelites to do with a man or woman who has done such an evil thing as worshiping other gods?
Response: The Israelites are to stone that person to death.

Question: How many witnesses are needed to convict a person to death?
Response: Two or three witnesses are needed to convict a person to death.

Question: Who must throw the first stones when putting a person to death?
Response: The witnesses must be the first to throw the stones, followed by the rest of the people of Israel.

Question: Why must the Israelites stone the person to death who has done this evil action of worshiping other gods?
Response: This will get remove this evil from among them.

Question: Who is to judge if the Israelites have a matter that is too hard for them to judge?
Response: They must go to the place that Yahweh will choose, and explain the case to the Levites who are serving as priests and to the judge who is serving at that time and they will make the decision.

Question: What man must die in order to remove the evil from Israel?
Response: Anyone who acts arrogantly and does not listen to the decision of the priest or judge, must die to remove the evil from Israel.

Question: How will the Israelites decide who to set up as a king over them at the time when they are in the land?
Response: Israelites will select from among their fellow Israelites someone whom Yahweh will choose.

Question: Why must the king of Israel not own multiple horses, nor send anyone back to Egypt to buy more horses?
Response: He must not send anyone back to Egypt to buy more horses for him because Yahweh has told the Israelites to never return to Egypt.

Question: Why is the king told not to have many wives?
Response: He is not to marry a lot of wives because they might cause his heart to turn away from Yahweh.

Question: What is the king instructed to do so that he may learn to honor Yahweh his God, and keep and obey all of his law?
Response: He must write for himself a copy of this law on a scroll from which he is to read every day of his life.

Question: Why is the king instructed to keep the scroll of the law with him?
Response: He must read from the scroll daily so that he does not think he is more important than his fellow Israelites, and so that he will completely obey the commandments.

Question: Why would the Levites be allowed to eat from the offerings given to Yahweh?
Response: The Levites would be able to eat the offerings because they would not receive land as an inheritance like the other Israelites.

Question: What was to be given to the priests from the sacrifices of the people?
Response: From the sacrifices of the people, the priests were to be given the shoulder, two cheeks, and stomach of the oxen and sheep and the first parts of their grain, wine, oil, and fleece of the sheep.

Question: Why were the priests supposed to receive these portions from the sacrifices of the people?
Response: They were to receive these portions because Yahweh chose them from among the tribes to serve as priests in his name.

Question: Which Levites can serve in the place that Yahweh will choose?
Response: Any Levite who wants to can serve as a priest in the place that Yahweh will choose.

Question: What were some of the disgusting practices followed by the other nations in the land?
Response: Some of the disgusting practices were sacrificing their children, using divination, practicing magic, using sorcery, and talking with the dead or spirits.

Question: Why will Yahweh drive out the nations from the land?
Response: Yahweh will drive out the nations from the land because he hates the disgusting things they do and he wants his people to stay away from those things.

Question: Who did Yahweh promise to raise up from among the people?
Response: Yahweh promised he would raise up a prophet like Moses for them to obey.

Question: When must a prophet be executed?
Response: A prophet must be executed if he speaks a prophecy in Yahweh’s name that Yahweh has not commanded him to speak, or he speaks in the name of other gods.

Question: What does it mean if a prophecy spoken in Yahweh’s name does not happen?
Response: If a prophecy does not happen, then the prophecy is something that Yahweh has not spoken.

Question: Why did Yahweh instruct the people to select three cities?
Response: He instructed the people to select three cities so that everyone who kills another person could flee to one of them for safety.

Question: What provision does Yahweh make for the person who kills another person by accident?
Response: Yahweh provided three cities to which that person could flee and live.

Question: Why should the one who kills another flee to a safe city?
Response: He should flee so that the relative of the victim does not pursue him in anger and kill him.

Question: What would be the conditions for adding three more cities for people to escape to?
Response: If Yahweh enlarged their borders, then they must add three more cities for people to escape to.

Question: What should happen if a person ambushes and kills someone and then flees to one of these cities?
Response: The elders of this man’s city must bring him back and turn him over to the victim’s relative so that the attacker may be executed.

Question: How many witnesses are needed for someone to be punished for their sin?
Response: There must be two or three witnesses for anyone to be punished for their sin.

Question: What should be done when controversy exists between two men?
Response: Then the two men who have a controversy must go before the priests and the judges who are serving at that time. The judges will inquire thoroughly to see if the witness falsely testified against the other.

Question: What must be done if a witness is found to be false?
Response: If the testimony of a witness is found to be false, then they must do to him as he had planned to do to the person he accused.

Question: Why must the people of Israel not be afraid when they go out to battle against their enemies?
Response: The people of Israel must not be afraid because Yahweh their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, will be with them.

Question: What should the priests do when the people of Israel draw near to the battle?
Response: The priest should speak to the people and tell them not to be afraid because Yahweh is with them to fight for them.

Question: What should a man do if he has built a new house and not yet dedicated it?
Response: He should return to his house so he does not die in battle and another man dedicate it.

Question: What should a man do if he is engaged to a woman and not yet married her?
Response: He should return and marry her so he does not die in battle and another man marry her.

Question: What was to happen if the people in a city far from Israel accepted the Israelites’ offer of peace?
Response: All the people found in that city were to become forced labor for the Israelites and serve them.

Question: What must happen if the city does not accept the offer of peace, but instead makes war against Israel?
Response: Israel must attack it and when Yahweh gives Israel victory they must kill every man in it.

Question: Why must the people of Israel utterly destroy everything that breathes in the cities of the land which Yahweh will give them as an inheritance?
Response: They must destroy everything so that the people of the land do not teach them to do disgusting things for their gods, which would cause them to sin against Yahweh.

Question: What should Israel do with the trees in the cities which they have under siege?
Response: Israel should not cut down the trees that are used for food, but they can cut down the trees that are not used for food to build structures for attacking the city.

Question: What should happen if someone who has been killed is found lying in a field and it is not known who killed him?
Response: The elders and judges should measure to determine the nearest city to where the body was found.

Question: Why are the priests to be consulted to settle disputes involving injuries?
Response: The priests are to be consulted and their decision to be accepted because Yahweh has chosen them to serve him in this way.

Question: What ritual must the elders of the city nearest to the killed man perform so that this murder will be forgiven?
Response: The elders of that city must wash their hands over the heifer whose neck was broken and declare that the people did not do this murder and Yahweh will not consider them guilty.

Question: What is an Israelite man to do if he sees a woman among the captives who he wants as his wife?
Response: If a man sees a woman among the captives who he wants as his wife, he is to bring her home where she will shave her head, cut her nails, and change her clothes. He is to give her a month to mourn for her parents and then he can marry her.

Question: How is an Israelite man to treat a captive that he had taken as a wife if he is no longer pleased with her?
Response: He must let her go rather than sell her for money.

Question: If a man has two wives, and he favors the one who has borne him his firstborn son, how must he distribute his inheritance?
Response: This man must recognize the firstborn son of the wife he does not favor by giving him a double portion of all that he possesses.

Question: What must the parents of a rebellious son do if the son will not obey them?
Response: They must bring that rebellious son to the elders of the city.

Question: What must the parents of the rebellious son say to the elders of his city?
Response: The parents of the rebellious son must tell the elders that their son is stubborn and rebellious and does not listen to them.

Question: Why must the men of the city respond by stoning the son to death?
Response: They must stone the son to death to remove the evil from among them, and cause all Israel to hear of it and fear.

Question: What must happen if a man commits a sin worthy of death and is hanged on a tree?
Response: The man’s body must not remain all night on the tree, but must be buried the same day so the land will not be defiled.

Question: What are they to do when they would see a stray animal of a fellow Israelite?
Response: They are to return the animal to its owner. If the owner is not nearby or they do not know who he is, they should take the animal to their own house until the owner comes searching for it and then return it to him.

Question: What did Moses tell them to do when they see an animal of a fellow Israelite fallen down in the road?
Response: They must help their fellow Israelite lift it up again.

Question: What is an abomination to Yahweh?
Response: It is an abomination to Yahweh for a woman to wear men’s clothing or a man to wear women’s clothing.

Question: What should the people of Israel do if they find a mother in a bird’s nest with her young or sitting on the eggs?
Response: They must let the mother go, but the young they may take for themselves.

Question: What is required when an Israelite builds a house?
Response: It is required that he put a railing around the roof so he would not be guilty of causing someone’s death if anyone fell from it.

Question: What would happen if the Israelites plant their vineyards with two kinds of seeds?
Response: If they plant their vineyards with two kinds of seeds, all the produce of the seed and the yield of the vineyard would have to be set apart.

Question: What were the Israelites instructed to make for the four corners of their garments?
Response: They were instructed to make tassels for the four corners of the garments with which they covered themselves.

Question: What must happen if a man has made a false accusation against his wife saying she was not a virgin?
Response: The elders of the city must punish the man. They must fine him 100 silvers and give it to the girl’s father. Also, she shall remain his wife, and he can never send her away.

Question: What should happen if the proof of the wife’s virginity is not found?
Response: If they cannot find proof of her virginity, then she must be brought out and stoned to death because she has done a disgraceful thing.

Question: What must happen if a man is found lying with a woman married to another man?
Response: Both the man and the woman must be put to death to make sure no one else from Israel does this evil action.

Question: Why are the people of Israel instructed to stone to death both a girl who is engaged to a man and another man if they are found lying together?
Response: They are instructed to stone both of them because the girl did not cry out and the man defiled the one who is engaged to be the wife of a fellow Israelite.

Question: What must happen if the man forces the engaged girl to lie with him when they are out in a field?
Response: If this happens out in a field, then the man must die, but nothing must be done to the girl because he found her out in the open countryside and even though she cried out, there was no one there to rescue her.

Question: What must happen if a man lies with a girl who is a virgin, but who is not engaged and they are discovered?
Response: The man who lay with her must give 50 silvers to the girl’s father, and she shall become his wife and he may never divorce her.

Question: What must a man not take from his father as his own?
Response: A man must not take his father’s wife as his own.

Question: Who is not allowed to enter the assembly of Yahweh?
Response: A man whose male organ is crushed or cut off, or a child from intermarriage and that person’s descendants to the tenth generation, are not allowed to enter the assembly of Yahweh.

Question: Why are Ammonites and Moabites not allowed to enter the assembly of Yahweh?
Response: They are not allowed to enter because they did not provide the Israelites with food and water when the Israelites came out of Egypt, and because they hired Balaam to curse the Israelites.

Question: What evidence showed that Yahweh did not listen to Balaam?
Response: Yahweh turned Balaam’s curse into a blessing for Israel.

Question: Why should they not detest the Edomites and Egyptians?
Response: They are not to detest the Edomites because they are considered relatives of the Israelites as they are descendants of Isaac. The Egyptians should not be detested because the Israelites once lived as foreigners in Egypt.

Question: If a man in the Israelite army becomes unclean because of what happens during the night, what must he do to become clean and return to the camp?
Response: He must go outside the camp until evening. Then he must bathe in water, and at sunset he can return to the camp.

Question: Why did Yahweh give instructions to the people of Israel about where and how to relieve themselves?
Response: Yahweh was walking in the midst of their camp, so the camp must be holy in order that Yahweh would not see any unclean thing and turn away from them.

Question: What are the instructions concerning a slave who escapes from their master?
Response: Such a slave is not to be returned to their master. They are to be allowed to live among the Israelites in any town they choose, and they must not be mistreated.

Question: Why were male and female cultic prostitutes not allowed among the Israelites, and why were their earnings not allowed to be brought as a vow into Yahweh’s house?
Response: These practices and the earnings from them are considered an abomination to Yahweh.

Question: How is the policy for charging interest different for fellow Israelites compared to foreigners?
Response: Israelites are not to charge interest to other Israelites on loans of money, food, or anything else. However, they are allowed to charge interest to foreigners.

Question: How important is it for an Israelite to fulfill a vow made to Yahweh?
Response: It is very important. An Israelite must not delay in fulfilling a vow made to Yahweh because Yahweh requires it, and failing to do so is a sin. They must do what they have voluntarily promised.

Question: What are Israelites allowed to do when passing through a neighbor’s vineyard or grain field?
Response: When in a neighbor’s vineyard, they are allowed to eat as many grapes as they want but not to put any in a container to take with them. When in a neighbor’s grain field, they are allowed to pluck ears of grain by hand but not to use a sickle to cut the stalks.

Question: What can a man who finds some indecent thing in his wife do to divorce her?
Response: If a man has found some indecent thing in his wife, he can write her a letter of divorce, give the paper to her, and send her out of his house where she can go and become another man’s wife.

Question: What does Yahweh not allow if the second husband writes her a letter of divorce and sends her out of the house or if he dies?
Response: Yahweh does not allow for the first husband who sent her away to take her again to be his wife.

Question: What are the benefits for a man who has taken a new wife?
Response: He does not have to go to war or be commanded to go on forced duty, but he can stay home for one year to make his new wife happy.

Question: Why were they not allowed to take a mill or an upper millstone as a pledge from another?
Response: They were not to take a millstone as a pledge because it would be taking a man’s livelihood away from him.

Question: What are the circumstances when a man would be declared a thief and must die?
Response: A man would be declared a thief who must die if he kidnaps a fellow Israelite and treats him as a slave or sells him.

Question: What word of caution from Yahweh about leprosy did Moses pass on to the people of Israel?
Response: Moses told them to carefully observe and follow every instruction which Yahweh gave to the priests regarding any plague of leprosy.

Question: What restrictions were given when collecting a pledge on a loan made to a neighbor?
Response: They were not to go into the house to get the pledge, but stand outside and wait for the man who received the loan to bring out the pledge.

Question: What must happen if a man gives his cloak as a pledge because he is a poor man?
Response: If the poor man gives his cloak as a pledge for a loan, the one who gives him the loan must not keep the cloak overnight, but must take it back to him by the time the sun goes down so he may sleep in his cloak.

Question: What must they do for a hired servant who is poor and needy?
Response: They must give him his wages every day because he is poor and is counting on it.

Question: How did Yahweh make it clear that everyone was responsible for their own sins?
Response: Yahweh told the people of Israel that the parents must not be put to death for the sins of their children and the children must not be put to death for the sins of their parents.

Question: Why were the people of Israel reminded about their time of slavery in Egypt and how Yahweh rescued them from there?
Response: They were reminded so that they would follow Yahweh’s commands about how to treat foreigners, orphans, and widows.

Question: What were the people of Israel commanded to do when they harvested their field or shook their olive tree?
Response: When they harvested their crops, they were not to go back for a forgotten sheaf in the field or to beat their olive tree a second time to get more olives, but to leave them for the foreigner, the fatherless, and the widow.

Question: What will a judge do when he reaches a judgement in a dispute between two men in court?
Response: He will declare the righteous as innocent and declare the wicked as guilty.

Question: Why will a judge limit the number of blows a man will receive?
Response: The judge will order the number of blows to not exceed 40 blows so the man will not be humiliated.

Question: What safeguard did Yahweh institute so that the name of a man would not disappear from Israel?
Response: If a man dies without having a son, his brother must take the dead man’s widow as his wife so that the firstborn she bears will carry on the name of that man’s dead brother.

Question: What punishment did Yahweh allow if the man’s brother did not wish to take his brother’s wife?
Response: The brother’s wife will take off his sandal in the presence of the elders, spit in his face, and tell him that this is what is done when a man refuses to preserve his brother’s family line.

Question: What would cause a wife to have one of her hands cut off?
Response: If two men were fighting and the wife of one of the men grabs the private part of the other man in an attempt to rescue her husband, then her hand must be cut off.

Question: What kind of dishonest act is disgusting to Yahweh?
Response: It is disgusting to Yahweh when a man has a large and a small weight to measure rather than a correct weight.

Question: Why must the people of Israel wipe out the memory of Amalek?
Response: Amalek did not honor God when Amalek met Israel on the road and attacked those who were faint and weary at the rear as they came out of Egypt.

Question: What must the people of Israel do when they possess the land which Yahweh has given them?
Response: The people of Israel must take some of the first of all the harvest of the land in a basket and go to the place Yahweh has chosen for them to worship.

Question: What must they say to the priest when they give him the basket?
Response: They must tell the priest that they declare to Yahweh that they have come and settled in the land that he swore to give to their ancestors.

Question: What must they say about their ancestor?
Response: The people of Israel must say that their ancestor was a wandering Aramean.

Question: How did the Egyptians treat the Israelites when they were in Egypt?
Response: The Egyptians treated the Israelites badly and afflicted them, making them do the work of slaves.

Question: How did Yahweh bring Israel out of Egypt?
Response: He brought them out with a mighty hand, with a display of his great fearsome power, with signs, and with wonders.

Question: What is the purpose for setting down the basket before Yahweh?
Response: The purpose is to worship Yahweh and rejoice in all the good that he has done for Israel, their families, the Levites, and the foreigners among them.

Question: What must they do after they have finished giving all the tithe from their harvest?
Response: They must give it to the Levites, to the foreigners, to the orphans, and to the widows so they may eat and be filled.

Question: What do the people of Israel want Yahweh to do for them?
Response: The people of Israel want Yahweh to look down from heaven and bless them and the land which he has given to them.

Question: What is Yahweh commanding the people to do?
Response: He is commanding them to obey his statues and decrees with all their heart and soul.

Question: What have the people declared before Yahweh?
Response: They have declared that Yahweh is their God and that they will walk in his ways, keep his commandments, and listen to and obey all that he tells them to do.

Question: What has Yahweh declared to the people of Israel?
Response: Yahweh has declared that Israel is his own possession and he will set them high above all the other nations in respect to praise, reputation, and honor so they will be a people that is set apart and holy to him.

Question: What did Moses and the elders of Israel command the people of Israel to keep?
Response: Moses and the elders of Israel told the people to keep all the commandments that Moses had given them that day.

Question: What were the people to do after they cross the Jordan River to the land which Yahweh would give them?
Response: They were to erect some large stones covered with plaster.

Question: What must the people build on Mount Ebal?
Response: The people must build an altar of stone to Yahweh without using iron tools to sacrifice burnt offerings to him.

Question: What were the people to write on the stones they had plastered?
Response: They were to write all the words of the law on the stones very plainly.

Question: Why was it important for the people of Israel to hear the voice of Yahweh and obey his commandments that day?
Response: It was important because on that day they became the people of Yahweh.

Question: Why were six of the tribes of Israel commanded to stand on Mount Gerizim?
Response: Moses told them that six of the tribes of Israel must stand on Mount Gerizim to receive the blessings that the Levites proclaimed.

Question: Why were six of the tribes of Israel commanded to stand on Mount Ebal?
Response: Moses told them that the other six tribes of Israel must stand on Mount Ebal to receive the curses that the Levites proclaimed.

Question: What is one thing that is detestable to Yahweh?
Response: One thing that is detestable to Yahweh is a carved or cast figure made by a craftsman who sets it up to worship it in secret.

Question: Why is a man cursed if he lies with his father’s wife?
Response: A man is cursed if he sleeps with his father’s wife, because he has violated his father’s marriage rights.

Question: What did the Levites say would happen to the man who secretly kills his neighbor or takes a bribe to kill an innocent person?
Response: That the man would be cursed.

Question: Why did the Levites say a man should keep the words of this law by obeying them?
Response: A man should obey the words of this law so he would not be cursed.

Question: What did the people of Israel have to do in order to be blessed by Yahweh and to be set above all the other nations of the earth?
Response: The people of Israel needed to listen carefully to the voice of Yahweh and obey all his commandments.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the people of Israel if they will obey his commandments?
Response: Yahweh will enable them to defeat their enemies.

Question: What does Moses tell the people of Israel they will become if they obey Yahweh’s commandments?
Response: Yahweh will establish them as his own holy people.

Question: What will Yahweh do for the people of Israel if they will obey his commandments?
Response: Yahweh will send rain for their land at the right time and bless all their work.

Question: What will happen to the people of Israel if they will not listen to the voice of Yahweh and keep all his commandments?
Response: All the curses will come upon them.

Question: What will happen to the skies and the earth if the Israelites do not obey the commandments?
Response: The skies will not give rain and the earth will be as dry and hard as iron.

Question: What will happen to Israel when they fight their enemies if they do not obey the commandments?
Response: They will flee from their enemies and be defeated by them.

Question: What will Yahweh cause to happen to the people of Israel if they are disobedient?
Response: He will cause them to have boils, ulcers, rash, and itch, as well as madness, blindness, and mental confusion.

Question: What will happen if an Israelite is betrothed to a woman, builds a house, or plants a vineyard but disobeys Yahweh?
Response: Another man will rape his betrothed and he will not be able to live in the house or enjoy the fruit of the vineyard.

Question: What will happen to the sons and daughters of the Israelites if they disobey Yahweh?
Response: Their sons and daughters will be given to other peoples and they will not return to them.

Question: What will the people of Israel become among all the peoples?
Response: They will become a horror, a proverb, and a taunt among all the peoples.

Question: How will the disobedience of the people of Israel affect the crops which they plant?
Response: They will put seed in their fields and plant vineyards but they will not enjoy the harvest or fruit from them because the worms and the locusts will eat them.

Question: How will the foreigner become more important than the people of Israel?
Response: Foreigners will lend to them while the people of Israel will not be able to lend to the foreigners so will be considered inferior to them.

Question: What will happen because the people of Israel did not listen to the voice of Yahweh and keep his commandments?
Response: All the curses listed will happen to them until they are destroyed.

Question: Why will the people of Israel serve the enemies of Yahweh?
Response: They will serve their enemies because they did not serve Yahweh with joyfulness and gladness of heart when they had abundant blessings.

Question: What did Moses prophesy about the people of Israel?
Response: Yahweh would bring a nation from far away to attack them with no respect for the aged or young, as well as taking their crops and animals, leaving them to starve.

Question: During the siege of the city what will the man who is described not share with even his own family?
Response: He will not give to any of them the flesh of his own children that he is going to eat.

Question: During the siege of the city what will a woman do with her own newborn and the children whom she will bear?
Response: During the siege of the city a woman will eat her own children whom she will bear.

Question: What will happen if the people of Israel do not honor the name of Yahweh by keeping the words in the book of the law?
Response: They will experience terrible plagues and enduring sicknesses.

Question: How many of the people of Israel will be left after Yahweh punishes them?
Response: Although they had been numerous, only a few of them will remain scattered among the other nations.

Question: Will the people of Israel find rest when they are scattered among the nations?
Response: They will find no peace or rest and they will be afraid, hopeless, and mournful.

Question: With whom did Yahweh make a covenant?
Response: Yahweh made a covenant with Israel in the land of Moab.

Question: What previous covenant had Yahweh made with the people of Israel?
Response: Yahweh previously made a covenant with Israel at Horeb.

Question: Of what did Moses remind all the people of Israel?
Response: Moses reminded all them of everything that Yahweh had done in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh, to all his servants, and to all his land.

Question: Why did Moses say that the people of Israel needed to be reminded of the great sufferings that their eyes had seen as well as the signs and wonders?
Response: Moses said that until that time, Yahweh had not given them understanding about those things.

Question: Why did Yahweh lead Israel for 40 years in the wilderness where their clothes and sandals did not wear out and they did not eat any bread or drink any wine or alcoholic drinks?
Response: He did those things so that they might know that he is Yahweh their God.

Question: What did Israel need to do in order to prosper in everything?
Response: They needed to keep the words of this covenant so that they might prosper in everything that they did.

Question: Before whom were all of the Israelites standing?
Response: All of the Israelites were standing before Yahweh their God.

Question: Why were the Israelites standing before Yahweh their God?
Response: They were standing before Yahweh in order to enter into the covenant that Yahweh was making with them on that day.

Question: What did Yahweh want to do for the people of Israel through this covenant?
Response: He wanted to make the people of Israel into a people for himself as he had promised their ancestors.

Question: What people were included in the covenant and oath that Yahweh was making with Israel that day in Moab?
Response: Everyone standing that day in Moab and also those who were not there with them were included in the covenant that Yahweh was making with Israel.

Question: About what were the people of Israel being reminded again?
Response: They were being reminded how they had lived in the land of Egypt and how they had traveled through the land that belonged to other people groups after they came out of Egypt.

Question: Why were the people being reminded of the disgusting things they had seen when they left Egypt?
Response: They were being reminded of the disgusting things they had seen so that there would not be any among them whose heart would turn away from Yahweh, worshiping the gods of those nations.

Question: What should the person who hears the words of the curse not say in his heart?
Response: The person who hears the words of the curse should not say that he will have peace even though he stubbornly does what he wants.

Question: What would happen to the man who says that he will have peace even though he stubbornly does what he wants?
Response: Yahweh will not forgive him, Yahweh will be angry with him and all the curses written in the book will happen to him and Yahweh will destroy him and his family forever.

Question: What will the future generations and other nations say when they see the plagues on this land and the diseases which Yahweh has sent on them?
Response: They will say that Yahweh did this to this land and wonder why he was so angry with the people who lived there.

Question: How will people reply when future generations ask why Yahweh did this to the land?
Response: The people will say that Yahweh became very angry and brought these curses on their land because they had abandoned the covenant they had made with Yahweh and because they went and worshiped other gods, gods that they had not worshiped before and that he had told them not to worship.

Question: What will the people say that Yahweh will do in his anger?
Response: The people will say that Yahweh, in his extreme anger, has taken them out of their land and thrown them into another land.

Question: To whom do things that are revealed belong?
Response: The things that are revealed belong forever to the people of Israel and to their descendants so that they may do all the words of the law.

Question: What must the people of Israel do so that Yahweh will rescue them from their captivity and have compassion on them and gather them from all the peoples where Yahweh had scattered them?
Response: They must return to Yahweh and obey his voice, follow all that he has commanded them with all their heart and with all their soul.

Question: From where will Yahweh gather Israel’s scattered people?
Response: Yahweh will gather Israel’s scattered people from the most distant places on earth.

Question: To what place will Yahweh bring the scattered Israelites?
Response: Yahweh will bring them into the land that their ancestors possessed.

Question: Why will Yahweh change the hearts of the Israelites and their descendants?
Response: Yahweh will change their hearts so that they will love Yahweh their God with all their heart and with all of their soul and so that they could live.

Question: On whom will Yahweh put the curses that had been on the people of Israel?
Response: Yahweh will put the curses on their enemies and on those who hate the people of Israel and have oppressed them.

Question: What will indicate that the people of Israel have returned to Yahweh?
Response: They will obey the voice of Yahweh and will do all his commandments that Moses commanded them.

Question: What will Yahweh do when the people of Israel return to him?
Response: Yahweh will make them prosper and he will rejoice over the people of Israel as he rejoiced over their fathers.

Question: What do the people of Israel need to do so that Yahweh will rejoice over them?
Response: The people of Israel need to obey the voice of Yahweh their God, keep his commandments and statutes that were written in the book of the law and turn to Yahweh with all their heart and all their soul.

Question: What does Moses say is not too hard for the people or too far for them to reach?
Response: Moses says that the commandments that he is giving to the people of Israel are not too hard for them to know and obey.

Question: What has Moses placed before the people of Israel?
Response: Moses is allowing them to choose life and good, or death and evil.

Question: What will happen if the people of Israel obey everything Yahweh their God has commanded them?
Response: They will live and multiply and Yahweh will bless them in the land where they were going.

Question: What will happen to the people if they do not listen and obey, but instead they bow down to other gods and worship them?
Response: They will surely perish and they will not live very long in the land.

Question: What is Moses asking the people of Israel to choose as he calls heaven and earth to witness?
Response: Moses is asking the people to choose life so that they and their descendants could live.

Question: Why should the people choose life?
Response: They should choose life so as to love Yahweh their God, to obey his voice and to cling to him because he is their life and will enable them and their descendants to live for a long time in the land that he has given to them.

Question: What did Moses tell all Israel about his advancing age?
Response: He told them that he was now 120 years old and that he could no longer go everywhere that they could go.

Question: What did Yahweh tell Moses about going over the Jordan?
Response: He told Moses that he would not be going over Jordan but God would go over Jordan ahead of them and would destroy the nations there for Israel.

Question: Who did Yahweh say would go over Jordan instead of Moses?
Response: Yahweh told Moses that Joshua would go over the Jordan to lead Israel.

Question: What had Yahweh done to Sihon and to Og, the kings of the Amorites?
Response: Yahweh had destroyed them and their land.

Question: What does Yahweh say he will do for Israel when they meet the nations in battle?
Response: Yahweh says that he will enable Israel to conquer them when Israel meets them in battle.

Question: What instruction does Moses give to the Israelites?
Response: Moses tells them to be strong and courageous, not to fear, and not to be afraid of the nations because Yahweh will go with them and will not fail or forsake them.

Question: What did Moses say to Joshua while of all Israel was listening?
Response: Moses told Joshua to be strong and courageous for Joshua would go with the people of Israel into the land that Yahweh swore to their ancestors to give to them and Yahweh will cause them to inherit the land.

Question: What did Moses tell Joshua that Yahweh would do for him?
Response: Moses told Joshua that Yahweh would go before Joshua and be with him, and Yahweh would not fail or abandon Joshua so he should not be afraid or dismayed.

Question: To whom did Moses give the law after he wrote it?
Response: Moses gave this law to the priests, the sons of Levi, who carried the box and he also gave copies of it to all the elders of Israel.

Question: How often did Moses command the priests to read the law that Moses gave to them?
Response: Moses commanded the priests to read the law at the end of every seven years, during the Festival of Huts, in the hearing of all Israel.

Question: Why did Moses tell the priests to assemble the men, women, children, and foreigners?
